sourceMovie.title: Coco
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Coco (2017) stuck because it commits to its family premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Lee Unkrich's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The family and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Trolls Band Together
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Coco clicked for you, Trolls Band Together (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Coco?
faq.answer: Try Trolls Band Together, Moana, Sing 2—each shares DNA with Coco while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Coco?
faq.answer: Coco (2017) is best described as Family, Animation, Music, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Coco part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Coco Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
